notes: Codex's TUI !cmd shell escape (e.g. !sleep 5) runs a command locally with no LLM round-trip, but UNLIKE claudecode it synthesizes a complete turn in the rollout — task_started, a user_message whose content is a <user_shell_command> block, and task_complete — so the daemon DOES drive a ready→working→ready arc per escape (intent reading: no stick in working, no premature ready). We assert the arc topology and final ready, NOT a working dwell length: the codex parser skips task_started and codex flushes the synthetic user_message only when the command exits, so the recorded working dwell is debounce-bounded (~2s), not command-bounded — see assessment.json caveats. The recipe runs two escapes (!ls fast, !sleep 5 slow) then a normal prompt that proves the session is still alive and a real turn still drives ready→working→ready.

text: Session appears in ready after launch
A !cmd shell escape drives ready → working (the TUI holds the session working while the command runs)
When the shell command exits, state resolves back to ready — no stick in working
A normal user prompt after the shell escapes drives ready → working — proves the session is still alive
Final normal turn completes back to ready
